title: Metastatic Prostate Cancer Market Set for Robust Growth Through 2036, Driven by The Emergence of PARP Inhibitors and PSMA-targeted Radioligand Therapies | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The metastatic prostate cancer market is projected to experience robust growth through 2036, valued at USD 11.5 billion in 2025 across seven major markets. The market is transitioning from conventional androgen deprivation therapy toward combination-based, biomarker-driven approaches, with novel therapies such as PSMA-targeted radioligand therapies and PARP inhibitors reshaping the treatment paradigm. Key emerging drugs include 225Ac-PSMA-617, 177Lu-DOTA-rosopatamab, and cabozantinib, with multiple pharmaceutical companies advancing innovative treatments in clinical trials.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Developing 177Lu-DOTA-rosopatamab (TLX591), a leading radioligand antibody-drug conjugate in Phase III ProstACT Global trial with interim results expected soon.
Advancing mevrometostat (EZH2 inhibitor) targeting 2026 launch and TALZENNA (talazoparib) in combination therapies. Multiple late-stage candidates in development.
Developing cabozantinib (CABOMETYX) for mCRPC with planned FDA supplemental application in late 2024, representing emerging second-line treatment option.
Advancing opevesostat (MK-5684) for first-line mCRPC treatment and collaborating on multiple emerging therapies in the robust pipeline.
Marketing ERLEADA (apalutamide) with recent Phase 3 PROTEUS trial results demonstrating significant clinical benefits in prostate cancer treatment.
Collaborating with Eli Lilly on Lu-PNT2002, a promising third-line radioligand therapy candidate in the robust emerging pipeline.
Partnering on Lu-PNT2002 and involved in POINT Biopharma collaboration for advanced metastatic prostate cancer therapies.
Developing azetukalner (XEN1101) as an emerging therapy in the metastatic prostate cancer pipeline with significant market growth potential.
Leading developer of 225Ac-PSMA-617 and PLUVICTO, which achieved blockbuster status with ~USD 1.4 billion in global sales in 2024. Strong pipeline presence in emerging PSMA-targeted therapies.
Developing NUBEQA (darolutamide) with recent Phase II ARACOG study showing superior cognitive outcomes compared to enzalutamide, expanding competitive positioning.
Co-marketing XTANDI (enzalutamide), an established AR pathway inhibitor continuing to dominate across mHSPC and mCRPC settings.

title: Radiopharmaceuticals Market Size to Exceed USD 15.21 Billion By 2035 Rising Cancer Cases Boost the Market Demand
author: Precedence Research
description: The global radiopharmaceuticals market is projected to grow from USD 8.28 billion in 2026 to USD 15.21 billion by 2035, driven by rising cancer and cardiovascular disease incidence. North America dominates with 43.95% market share, while Asia-Pacific is expected to register the fastest growth. Therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals and Technetium-99m lead their respective segments, with hospitals and clinics as the primary end users.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Key player in the radiopharmaceuticals market with exposure to growing therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals segment and cancer treatment applications.
Specialized radiopharmaceutical company positioned to benefit from market expansion driven by rising cancer cases and nuclear medicine adoption.
Major pharmaceutical company listed among key radiopharmaceuticals market players, benefiting from growing oncology and precision medicine trends.
Healthcare distributor positioned to benefit from increased radiopharmaceutical production and distribution expansion.
Listed as a major company in the radiopharmaceuticals market, positioned to benefit from strong market growth projected at 7.31% CAGR through 2035.
Key provider of imaging technology infrastructure (PET/SPECT systems) essential for radiopharmaceutical diagnostics and treatment.

title: Lantheus Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Business Update
author: Na
description: Lantheus Holdings reported Q4 2025 revenue of $406.8 million and full-year revenue of $1.54 billion, with adjusted EPS of $1.67 and $6.08 respectively. The company announced a strategic refocus on PET radiodiagnostics and plans to pursue value-maximizing alternatives for radiotherapeutic assets. Despite PYLARIFY sales declining 9.7% in Q4, the company expects up to four FDA approvals in 2026 and provided FY2026 guidance of $1.4-1.45 billion in revenue and $5.00-5.25 adjusted EPS.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed results with modest revenue growth (4.0% Q4, 0.5% FY) but declining PYLARIFY sales (-9.7% Q4, -6.5% FY) offset by strategic acquisitions and pipeline expansion. Positive forward guidance and multiple FDA approvals expected in 2026, but the divestiture of legacy SPECT business and shift in strategy suggest operational restructuring. Adjusted EPS growth of 4.7% in Q4 is modest, and full-year adjusted EPS declined from $6.76 to $6.08.

title: PSMA-Targeted Therapy & Radiodiagnostics Competitor Analysis Report 2026: PYLARIFY Exceeds US$ 1 Billion in Net Sales and Became the First Ever Blockbuster Radiodiagnostic
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: PYLARIFY has become the first blockbuster radiodiagnostic, exceeding $1 billion in 2024 net sales with 24.3% growth. PLUVICTO, a radioligand therapeutic agent, posted $1.392 billion in 2024 sales with 42% growth. PSMA-targeted therapies and radiodiagnostics represent a clinically validated and commercially successful approach for prostate cancer treatment and diagnosis, with multiple radioligands in development.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: PYLARIFY achieved historic milestone as first blockbuster radiodiagnostic with $1.058 billion in 2024 sales and strong 24.3% year-over-year growth, demonstrating robust commercial success and market validation.
PLUVICTO generated $1.392 billion in 2024 sales with exceptional 42% growth, indicating strong market adoption and commercial momentum in the radioligand therapy space for prostate cancer treatment.

title: Lantheus to Host Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Conference Call and Webcast on February 26, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Time
author: Na
description: Lantheus Holdings announced it will host an earnings conference call and webcast on February 26, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. ET to discuss its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial and operating results. The company also recently closed the sale of its SPECT business to SHINE Technologies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article is primarily an announcement of an earnings call and webcast with no forward-looking statements or performance indicators. While the SPECT business sale closure is mentioned, it lacks detail on financial impact or strategic implications, making this a routine corporate announcement without clear positive or negative implications.
